title: Fix coverage + wide ternary bug
body:
Following on from #6150 which I borked somehow.

> As to questions above usually if an AssignDly is fractured it's correct to keep the same type, but I didn't look at it in detail yet so can't say for sure if that's right.

@wsnyder I did try out making the intermediate assignment always an ASSIGN instead of following the assignment type of the node that's being broken up.  That does fix my test (by producing the correct ordering in the C++) and doesn't appear to break anything else.

So, my "fix" appears to do everything I want but runs counter to your assertion.  Can you explain why we should follow the assignment type of the node which is being split up?  If that type is ASSIGNDLY doesn't that mean we're incorrectly pipelining the assignment by conjuring up a new non-blocking assignment that wasn't previously there?
